description: Mobile web phase A5: the unsupported-combination SKIP matrix on Android, asserted as behavior. Chrome is the one supported Android browser; every other engine SKIPs deterministically at the mobile-browser gate — before any SDK detection, toolchain install, or emulator boot — so these land SKIPPED on EVERY host, capable or not. Mixing native app surfaces and web steps in one mobile context is likewise a deterministic SKIP (deferred to the A6 vocabulary work). The FAIL path (device-fixed browser config like headless:false / window / viewport) is asserted in mocha (test/core-core.test.js), not here — fixtures must never FAIL.

tests.description: firefox isn't available on android — SKIPPED with the supported browser named.
webkit (the desktop engine) isn't available on android — SKIPPED.
safari on an android entry stays `safari` (no webkit aliasing on mobile pairs) and SKIPs on the matrix.
One context mixing a native app surface and a browser step — deterministic SKIP with a split-the-test pointer.
